definition: To reduce the seriousness, harmfulness or negative impact of a problem or risk.

sentences.text: The team installed an additional sensor to _____ the risk of an undetected failure.
Preventive maintenance can _____ the impact of unexpected equipment breakdowns.
We revised the robot path to _____ the possibility of a collision.

definition: Very strict, precise or demanding, especially regarding rules, standards or requirements.

sentences.text: The automotive customer imposed _____ quality requirements on every welded component.
The machine must comply with _____ safety standards before commissioning.
The inspection system was designed to meet the plant's _____ acceptance criteria.

definition: A difference between two results, values, statements or conditions that should normally agree.

sentences.text: The engineer found a _____ between the commanded position and the actual robot position.
A small _____ in the measurement data indicated a calibration problem.
We investigated the _____ between the production report and the PLC counter.

definition: The ability of different systems, devices or software applications to communicate and work together effectively.

sentences.text: Profinet improves _____ between the PLC, robots and field devices.
The project required reliable _____ between equipment from several manufacturers.
Poor protocol _____ caused delays during system integration.

definition: A possible future event or problem that must be prepared for, especially through an alternative plan.

sentences.text: The project manager prepared a _____ plan in case the new drive failed during commissioning.
We kept a spare sensor as a _____ for unexpected production failures.
Every critical installation should include a realistic _____ strategy.

definition: To correct a mistake, fault or unacceptable situation.

sentences.text: The technician modified the PLC logic to _____ the timing error.
We must _____ the safety fault before restarting the production line.
The supplier was asked to _____ the dimensional problem immediately.

definition: Strong, reliable and able to continue functioning under difficult or changing conditions.

sentences.text: The control system needs a _____ recovery procedure for communication failures.
We developed a _____ robot program that can handle minor product variations.
A _____ design reduces the risk of production interruptions.

definition: Possible and practical to achieve with the available time, resources and technology.

sentences.text: The proposed cycle time is technically _____ if the robot paths are optimized.
We evaluated whether the automation concept was financially _____.
The team selected the most _____ solution after comparing three alternatives.

definition: Causing harm, damage or a negative effect on something.

sentences.text: Excessive vibration can be _____ to the service life of the motor.
Frequent emergency stops may have a _____ effect on production efficiency.
Ignoring minor welding defects can be _____ to final product quality.

definition: To improve a process by making it simpler, faster or more efficient.

sentences.text: The new HMI screens helped _____ the operator's daily tasks.
We automated the reporting process to _____ production tracking.
The project team reorganized the workflow to _____ commissioning activities.
